title: Welcome to my CV Page
text:
Resumé

Econometrician and PhD. researcher at Systems and Industrial Engineering at PUCPR,
MSc in Economic Development from UFPR, with over 15 years of experience in data analysis, modeling, processing and presentation of information for intelligent decision making.


        



Academics

BackgroundCertificationsTeaching Experience








NoteHighlights









  
  
    
    
      2022 to 2027 (expected)
      PhD Candidate – Systems & Industrial Eng.
      Research in applied time series analysis, Financial Econometrics, Bayesian Econometrics, Portfolio Multiobjective Optimization, and Modelling Extremal Events.
      
        
          
        
      
      
        Visit our research project at PIBIC AgroFinance Page
      
    
  

  
  
    
    
      2011
      MSc. in Economic Development
      Course completed at UFPR with an emphasis on big data, multivariate analysis, and econometrics. Thesis available at acervodigital.ufpr.br.

  Feb 2025 – Jul 2026
  
    Undergraduate Professor
    FAE Business School – Curitiba, PR
    Taught undergraduate courses in Data Science for Business and Financial Markets at FAE University. Responsibilities included:
      
        Big Data for Finance Project – Applying data science and big data techniques to financial decision-making.
        Computational Programming – Fundamentals and applications in data analytics and business intelligence.
        Risk Management – Quantitative methods and practical tools for financial risk assessment and mitigation.
        Econometrics – Regression diagnostics, causal inference, panel and time-series methods, and policy/market applications.
        Project Appraisal and Investment Analysis – DCF modeling, NPV/IRR, sensitivity and scenario analysis, Monte Carlo simulation, and real options.

Consulting experience includes projects for leading organizations such as Grupo Fleury, Aurora Alimentos, and Cogna Educacional. Main areas of work include:

LAB.PR's core areas include: data science and artificial intelligence, plain language use and public document redesign, systems integration for high-precision public policies, technology skills development programs, and institutional partnerships. Under my leadership, the lab aims to transform state public management through the application of advanced technologies and data governance methodologies, promoting transparency and efficiency in public administration.

title: Rodrigo Hermont Ozon
text:
OQuant Data Science Consulting
  
  
    
  



OQuant is an independent data science consultancy founded in March 2025 by Rodrigo Hermont Ozon — economist, econometrician, and PhD researcher in Systems and Industrial Engineering. The premise is simple: business decisions deserve the same rigor as peer-reviewed research. OQuant combines econometric modeling, statistical validation, and modern AI engineering to deliver analyses you can defend in front of a board, an auditor, or a regulator — not black boxes, and not slideware.

title: Rodrigo Hermont Ozon
text: OQuant is an independent data science consultancy founded in March 2025 by Rodrigo Hermont Ozon — economist, econometrician, and PhD researcher in Systems and Industrial Engineering. The premise is simple: business decisions deserve the same rigor as peer-reviewed research. OQuant combines econometric modeling, statistical validation, and modern AI engineering to deliver analyses you can defend in front of a board, an auditor, or a regulator — not black boxes, and not slideware.

title: Rodrigo Hermont Ozon
text:
What OQuant does

  
    Forecasting & Time Series
    Demand, price, and volatility forecasting with classical econometrics (ARIMA, GARCH-family, GAMLSS, Bayesian MSGARCH) and machine learning hybrids — with honest out-of-sample validation, not curve-fitting.
  
  
    Econometric Modeling & Causal Inference
    Answering "what actually drives this?" — regression diagnostics, panel and instrumental-variable methods, quasi-experimental designs, and Bayesian multicausal models grounded in economic theory.
  
  
    Risk Analytics & Simulation
    Quantitative risk measurement and scenario analysis: Monte Carlo simulation, extreme value modeling, portfolio and multi-objective optimization for pricing, sourcing, and investment decisions.
  
  
    Machine Learning & Predictive Modeling
    Supervised and deep learning models (including CNN+LSTM architectures and survival analysis for predictive maintenance) built with proper feature engineering, ablation testing, and MLOps discipline.
  
  
    Business Intelligence & Decision Support
    KPI frameworks, dashboards, and analytical pipelines that turn scattered data into a single, trusted view — from data modeling and governance documentation to executive-ready reporting.
  
  
    Generative AI for Business
    Practical LLM applications: internal chatbots, multi-agent and sub-agent workflow orchestration, and AI-assisted knowledge management — designed around your processes, with measurable value.

title: Rodrigo Hermont Ozon
text:

    Grupo Fleury

    Cogna Educacional
    Consulting work for one of Brazil's largest education companies.
  

Client engagements are confidential; sector references are shared with permission and project details remain under NDA.

title: Rodrigo Hermont Ozon
text:
A disciplined engagement model

  
    Step 1
    Diagnostic
    Understand the decision, not just the data
    We start with the business question and the decision it must inform. I audit the available data, map the constraints, and define success criteria before a single model is fit. If the data cannot support the question, you hear that on week one — not at delivery.
  
  
    Step 2
    Modeling & validation
    Rigor before results
    Candidate models are built, stress-tested, and compared against honest baselines with out-of-sample and backtesting protocols. Assumptions are documented, uncertainty is quantified, and every result comes with its limitations stated plainly.
  
  
    Step 3
    Deployment & handoff
    Production code, not a PDF
    Deliverables are reproducible: versioned code, documented pipelines, and dashboards or APIs your team can own. I train your people so the solution outlives the engagement.
  
  
    Step 4
    Monitoring & advisory
    Models drift; decisions evolve
    Optional ongoing support: performance monitoring, model recalibration, and advisory sessions as new questions emerge — so the analytics keep pace with the business.

title: Rodrigo Hermont Ozon
text:
What you are actually buying

  
    PhD-level rigor
    Active doctoral research in multi-objective optimization, Bayesian econometrics, and time series at PUCPR, an MSc in Economic Development (UFPR), peer-reviewed publications, and reviewer roles for journals such as Applied Soft Computing. The methods behind your project are the ones surviving academic scrutiny.
  
  
    15+ years of industry track record
    Hands-on delivery inside large organizations — Volvo Group, BRF, CNH Industrial, Unimed — plus UNDP/UN research work and years of economic analysis for industry federations. I know how corporate data, politics, and deadlines actually behave.
  
  
    End-to-end capability
    From statistical theory to production code: econometric specification, Python/R/SQL implementation, cloud data pipelines (Azure, Databricks, PySpark), and MLOps. One consultant covers the full path from hypothesis to deployed system.

title: Rodrigo Hermont Ozon
text:
Let’s talk about your problem
A first conversation costs nothing and usually clarifies whether quantitative modeling can move your decision — and whether I am the right person to build it.

title: Rodrigo Hermont Ozon
text:
Econometrician · The original data science
    
    
      Data Scientist at Unimed do Estado do Paraná, PhD researcher in Industrial & Systems
      Engineering (PUCPR), founder of OQuant consulting and former professor at FAE Business School (2025–2026).
      Fifteen years applying GARCH-family models, Bayesian econometrics, multi-objective optimization
      and, more recently, multi-agent AI systems to problems in healthcare, agribusiness and industry.
    
    
      Consulting
      Download CV (PDF)
      Full CV
      WhatsApp

title: Rodrigo Hermont Ozon
text:
Four fronts, one discipline

  
    Econometrics & Forecasting
    Time series, Bayesian methods, GARCH-family volatility models, causal inference and
    multi-objective portfolio optimization — applied to commodities, healthcare and industry.
  
  
    Data Science & AI
    Machine learning, MLOps and Generative AI: internal chatbots, multi-agent orchestration
    and AI-assisted decision-making in production environments.
  
  
    Consulting (OQuant)
    Founder & principal consultant. Projects for Grupo Fleury, Aurora Alimentos and Cogna
    Educacional in forecasting, risk analytics and decision support.
  
  
    Teaching & Research
    Taught Data Science for Business and Financial Markets at FAE (2025–2026). PhD researcher at
    PUCPR. Reviewer for Applied Soft Computing and PRINCIPIA.

title: Rodrigo Hermont Ozon
text:
Career timeline


  
    Jan 2026 – Present
    Data Scientist
    Unimed do Estado do Paraná – Curitiba, PR
    Healthcare analytics, BI and Generative AI: dashboards and KPI monitoring, predictive modeling, internal chatbots and multi-agent orchestration supporting AI-assisted decision-making.
  

  
    Mar 2025 – Present
    Founder & Principal Consultant — Data Scientist
    OQuant Data Science Consulting – Remote
    Consulting in data science, econometric modeling, forecasting, causal inference, risk analytics and AI-based decision support, serving clients such as Grupo Fleury, Aurora Alimentos and Cogna Educacional.
  

  
    Feb 2025 – Jul 2026
    Undergraduate Professor
    FAE Business School – Curitiba, PR
    Courses in econometrics, risk management, computational programming, big data for finance, and project appraisal and investment analysis.
  

  
    Jan 2025 – Apr 2025
    Data Engineer Professional
    Volvo Group Digital & IT – Curitiba, PR
    Built data pipelines with SQL, Python and PySpark on Azure (ADF, Databricks), developed predictive models with agile teams, and applied MLOps practices for model monitoring.
  

  
    Oct 2024 – Dec 2024
    Data Scientist Area Coordinator
    LAB.PR – Curitiba, PR
    Coordinated the state public-intelligence and data science laboratory, applying AI, data governance and systems integration to make public administration more transparent and efficient.
  

  
    Apr 2023 – Sep 2024
    Data Science Consultant
    CNH Industrial – Curitiba, PR (Remote)
    Built and trained ML/AI models (TensorFlow, Keras, Torch) on complex heterogeneous data — time series and image processing — over a Data Lake stack (Hadoop, Spark, Kafka) on Microsoft Azure.
  

  
    May 2021 – Mar 2023
    Senior Data Scientist
    BRF – Curitiba, PR
    Managed BI & Analytics products; processed ~700 economic time series to build a predictive corn pricing model (CBOT and B3) and a Bayesian multicausal model of price drivers grounded in microeconometric literature.
  

  
    May 2021 – Jul 2021
    Researcher / Consultant
    UNDP Brazil – Curitiba, PR
    Automated data extraction from official public sources via APIs and built socio-economic dashboards for the UNDP BRA/18/024 project on reducing violence against children and adolescents in Brazil.
  

  
    Jan 2021 – Mar 2021
    Sr. BI Analyst (Data Scientist)
    Tecnofit – Curitiba, PR
    Implemented a data-driven culture: fitness-market studies with CAGED/RAIS data, automated extractions from the Hubspot CRM and Google Places APIs, OKR-aligned dashboards and data governance documentation.
  

  
    Oct 2020 – Dec 2020
    Sr. Data Scientist
    Deal Technologies – Curitiba, PR
    Exclusive consultant on the Market Intelligence and Pricing project for Rumo Logística: validated deliverables from two international consultancies, audited PostgreSQL data quality, and documented ETL automation pipelines to AWS S3.
  

  
    Feb 2020 – Mar 2020
    Market Intelligence Analyst
    ALCAST do Brasil S/A – Curitiba, PR
    Led market mapping, segmentation and risk/opportunity assessment, proposing the migration from spreadsheets to real-time dashboards in RMarkdown and Power BI.
  

  
    Nov 2019 – Oct 2020
    Freelance Data Scientist
    Upwork.com – Remote
    On-demand projects in data science, econometrics and scientific document editing with LaTeX and R.
  

  
    Oct 2019 – Nov 2019
    Business Intelligence Consultant
    Kietec (Knowledge Information and Technology) – Curitiba, PR
    Requirements gathering, predictive modeling proposals and advanced data analysis to improve decision-making.
  

  
    May 2019 – Oct 2019
    Sr. Statistics Analyst
    Grupo Bitcoin Banco – Curitiba, PR
    Systematized statistically measurable information across all departments, implemented real-time BI for big data visualization, and supported executive decisions with statistical reporting.
  

  
    Oct 2012 – Nov 2018
    Mid-Level Economic Analyst
    FIEP – Curitiba, PR
    Economic analysis of Paraná's main productive chains for business unions and collective bargaining; built Power BI databases of regional economic data and reviewed SENAI/SESI innovation proposals.
  

  
    2011 – 2021
    Economist — Projects & Fundraising
    ONG Acreditar e Compartilhar – Brazil
    Volunteer collaborator designing and submitting third-sector projects to public and private bids, teaching PMI-based project management for NGOs, and consulting for other nonprofits.
  

  
    Oct 2008 – Oct 2013
    Economist / Project Coordinator
    IBQP – Curitiba, PR
    Researcher on productive chains and innovation projects; coordinated the Municipal Development Index for Micro and Small Enterprises (ID-MPE) for SEBRAE in partnership with UFPR researchers.

title: Rodrigo Hermont Ozon
text:
MOST RECENT APPOINTMENT



  
  Feb 2025 – Jul 2026 · Curitiba, PR
  Taught undergraduate courses in Data Science for Business and Financial Markets:
  
    Big Data for Finance Project — data science and big data techniques applied to financial decision-making.
    Computational Programming — fundamentals and applications in data analytics and business intelligence.
    Risk Management — quantitative methods and practical tools for financial risk assessment and mitigation.
    Econometrics — regression diagnostics, causal inference, panel and time-series methods, and policy/market applications.
    Project Appraisal and Investment Analysis — DCF modeling, NPV/IRR, sensitivity and scenario analysis, Monte Carlo simulation, and real options.
  
  
    Visit the class site
